event: John de Courcy departs from Trim with 22 knights and 300 foot soldiers to begin the invasion of Ulster.
Meath Brigade IRA volunteers successfully ambush an RIC patrol at Trim during the War of Independence.
King Henry IV issues a royal decree ordering the urgent repair of the town walls of Trim due to damage from Gaelic forces.
The town of Kells receives its first Royal Murage Grant, allowing for the construction of defensive stone walls.
The Dean Cogan branch of the Irish National Foresters is established as a key local benefit society.
Charles Stewart Parnell is selected as the Home Rule candidate at a massive county rally, launching his political career.
The Battle of Ashbourne: Thomas Ashe and Richard Mulcahy lead the 5th Fingal Battalion to a major victory over the RIC during the Easter Rising.
The IRA's Meath Brigade executes a raid on the telegraph and telephone equipment at the Navan Post Office.
The 1798 Rebellion officially begins in County Meath with the uprising of United Irishmen.
Lambert Simnel, supported by Meath nobility, is crowned 'King Edward VI' in a challenge to the English throne.
The Battle of Tara Hill: A decisive defeat for the United Irishmen at the hands of the Reay Fencibles and Yeomanry.
King William III lands at Carrickfergus and begins his march toward the River Boyne in Meath.
Williamite forces reach the north bank of the River Boyne near Oldbridge to face the Jacobite army.
The Battle of the Boyne takes place (Julian Calendar date), the most significant battle in modern Irish history.
The Truce in the War of Independence comes into effect, halting major IRA operations in Meath.
The Gregorian anniversary of the Battle of the Boyne, marking the Jacobite retreat from Donore.
A massive tenant protest is held in Kells during the Great Famine, demanding security of tenure and fair rent.
The last recorded ancient Tailteann Games are held at Teltown under the patronage of High King Rory O'Connor.
Daniel O'Connell's 'Monster Meeting' at the Hill of Tara attracts hundreds of thousands to demand the Repeal of the Union.
Record numbers of visitors arrive via excursion trains for the Meath Agricultural Show.
The Drogheda to Navan railway line officially opens for passenger traffic.
An RIC sergeant is shot dead in an ambush by the IRA at Grangegeeth, Slane.
Irish rebel forces begin the Siege of Drogheda, sparking conflict across the Meath borderlands.
The Battle of Julianstown: Catholic rebels defeat a government force in a significant early victory of the 1641 Rebellion.
The Trim RIC Barracks is destroyed by fire during a daring raid by the IRA.
The Battle of Kells: Edward Bruce and his Scots-Irish army defeat Roger Mortimer, resulting in the sacking of the town.
The Midland Great Western Railway opens the Hill of Down station.
Winter Solstice: The sunrise illuminates the inner chamber of Newgrange, a precise astronomical event occurring for over 50 centuries.
